content.title: Asian Stocks Shrug Off Tariff Jitters
content.summary: Asian stocks rose broadly, with South Korea and Taiwan setting records, despite renewed trade uncertainty.  Hong Kong’s Hang Seng Index rose 2.5%.   South Korea’s Kospi composite closed at a record high, as did its biggest component, Samsung Electronics.

content.title: Indian Stocks Advance After U.S. Agrees to Slash Tariffs
content.summary: Indian stocks jumped after President Trump agreed to slash tariffs on the country. The U.S. is lowering levies on Indian goods to 18% after the two nations struck a trade pact and Trump said New Delhi, led by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, agreed to stop buying Russian oil.

content.title: EU-India Trade Deal by the Numbers
content.summary: That's the annual saving equivalent the European Union expects as a result of its newly struck trade deal with India.  The EU says the pact is set to eliminate or reduce tariffs for more than 90% of its goods.  It is the latest example of U.S. trading partners seeking to curb their reliance on America by expanding ties with other markets.

As you read on, you will see how to interpret these small price target moves and track the evolving analyst story around the stock. Analyst Price...

content.title: Bernstein: Human oversight remains critical despite rapid AI coding integration
content.summary: Investing.com -- Generative AI is not yet driving systematic job losses in the technology sector, according to a report from Bernstein, despite the rapid integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into professional coding workflows. Following 50 hours of intensive interviews with tech professionals globally, analysts found that AI is reshaping role boundaries, but human capital remains "surprisingly resilient," debunking popular narratives of an immediate white-collar "apocalypse."

content.title: India's TCS urging staff to use AI despite risk to revenue, CEO says
content.summary: Tata Consultancy Services is urging employees to use artificial intelligence tools ‌to deliver work faster and cheaper, even if it ‌eats into the company's revenue, the CEO of India's largest software-services provider ​said on Wednesday.  "We are telling ‌associates that if you ⁠find that you can do something faster, better, cheaper with AI, you should probably go ⁠and tell your customers, even if it cannibalises revenue," CEO K Krithivasan said at the Nasscom Technology and Leadership Forum in ​Mumbai.  TCS' stance mirrors that of smaller rival Wipro, which expects rapid AI adoption to ‌boost rather than shrink demand for ​software service providers.

thumbnail.caption: Illustration shows TCS: Tata Consultancy Services logo

content.title: ServiceNow AI Push Deepens Enterprise Role With Deals And Insider Buying
content.summary: ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) is accelerating its AI agenda with the acquisition of Pyramid Analytics to integrate advanced business analytics and AI into its platform. The deal follows earlier purchases of Data World and Veza and comes alongside fresh AI-focused partnerships with Tata Consultancy Services and Kearney. CEO Bill McDermott and other executives have canceled planned share sales and made open market share purchases, signaling confidence in the company during a period of industry concern...
